sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Juventus are in solid form, remaining unbeaten in their last 6 matches with 3 wins and 3 draws, showing attacking intent with an average of 2.03 expected goals per game in recent outings. Cagliari, on the other hand, are struggling, winless in their last 8 matches, managing only 3 draws and conceding first in 5 of their last 6 games. This contrast in momentum heavily favors Juventus going into this match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Juventus have dominated Cagliari in recent head-to-head encounters, winning 3 of the last 5 matchups and remaining unbeaten in their last 9 meetings. Their last two encounters resulted in a 4-0 win for Juventus at home and a 1-0 victory away, highlighting their control over Cagliari, who have failed to keep a clean sheet in these games.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Juventus rank 7th in Serie A with 15 goals scored and a strong defensive record, conceding only 11 goals, backed by high possession (58.6%) and effective final third entries. Cagliari sit 14th, with just 12 goals scored and 17 conceded, struggling to convert chances and lacking defensive stability. Juventus’ superior stats across key metrics further underline their advantage.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Juventus, Kenan Yıldız has been a standout with 2 goals and 3 assists this season, while Dušan Vlahović remains a constant threat up front. Cagliari will rely on Gennaro Borrelli, who has 3 goals, to spark their attack, but missing key defender Yerry Mina could weaken their backline against Juventus’ potent offense.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Juventus’ unbeaten streak, historical dominance over Cagliari, and superior season stats, they are clear favorites, aligning with Sofascore’s 64% probability of a home win. Cagliari’s poor form and defensive frailties make an upset unlikely, though they might aim for a draw if they can capitalize on late chances, as seen in their goal distribution.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Allianz Stadium saw Juventus take a narrow 2-1 lead over Cagliari. Juventus dominated much of the game with higher possession and more entries into the final third and penalty area. Their attacking intent paid off with two goals from Kenan Yıldız, who has been the standout performer for the home side. Cagliari, however, managed to respond quickly after conceding the first goal, with Sebastiano Esposito finding the net to keep them in contention. Despite Juventus' overall control, Cagliari showed moments of resilience, particularly in defensive duels, though they struggled to create consistent chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: In terms of attacking momentum, Juventus started the match strongly, maintaining consistent pressure in the early stages with frequent attacking spells. Their persistence in the final third was evident throughout most of the half. Cagliari had brief periods where they pushed forward, especially around the middle of the half, managing to shift the momentum temporarily in their favor. However, Juventus regained control in the latter stages, showing a significant surge in attacking intent towards the end of the half, culminating in their second goal. Cagliari's attacking efforts were sporadic, often stifled by Juventus' defensive organization.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: For Juventus, Kenan Yıldız has been exceptional, scoring both goals and posing a constant threat in attack. His ability to find space and convert chances has been pivotal. Pierre Kalulu also contributed significantly with an assist and key passes that created dangerous opportunities. On the Cagliari side, Sebastiano Esposito stood out with his well-taken goal, showing composure in front of goal. Marco Palestra also made an impact with an assist and strong defensive work, winning multiple tackles to disrupt Juventus' rhythm.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Juventus have controlled the game through their dominance in possession and ability to win duels, especially in the air, which has allowed them to maintain pressure in Cagliari's half. Their frequent entries into the penalty area suggest they are likely to keep pushing for more goals. Cagliari, on the other hand, have been more cautious, focusing on counter-attacks, though their limited final third presence indicates a struggle to break through Juventus' defense. The away side will need to be more clinical if they are to level the scoreline.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Areas for improvement
halftimeSections.text: Juventus could look to capitalize even more on their territorial advantage by converting their chances into goals, as they missed opportunities despite creating significant openings. Defensively, they need to be wary of Cagliari's quick transitions, as seen with Esposito's goal. For Cagliari, the key will be to increase their presence in the final third and take advantage of any defensive lapses from Juventus. They also need to shore up their aerial duels, where they have been notably weaker.
